title: Inventory tag with scoped enum and class template
prompt_md:
## Goal
Build a small system that combines a class template with a scoped enum. A `Tagged<T>` struct holds a `Category` (a scoped enum with values `food`, `tool`, `book`) and a value of type `T`. Read a category number and a value from input, store them in a `Tagged<int>`, and print the category name and the value.

## Input
A single line containing two integers separated by a space. The first integer is `0`, `1`, or `2` (category code: 0=food, 1=tool, 2=book). The second is an arbitrary integer (the value).

## Output
One line: `<name>: <value>` followed by a newline, where `<name>` is `food`, `tool`, or `book` depending on the input category.

## Requirements
1. Define `enum class Category { food, tool, book };` at file scope.
2. Define `template <typename T> struct Tagged { Category category; T value; };` at file scope.
3. Read two `int` values from `std::cin`; convert the first to `Category` using `static_cast<Category>(...)`.
4. Construct a `Tagged<int>` from the category and the value.
5. Print `<name>: <value>` where `<name>` is determined by a `switch` on the category. End with a newline.
6. You may assume the first input is always 0, 1, or 2.

## Notes
This exercise pulls together scoped enums (13.2/13.6), structs (13.7), and class templates (13.13).

title: Complete sumPair with an alias template
prompt_md:
## Goal
Complete the function `sumPair` so it returns the sum of the two members of a `SameTypePair<int>`. The class template `Pair`, the alias template `SameTypePair`, and `main` are already provided. `main` reads two integers, builds a `SameTypePair<int>` using CTAD, calls `sumPair`, and prints the result.

## Input
A single line containing two integers separated by a space.

## Output
One line containing the sum of the two integers, followed by a newline.

## Requirements
1. Implement the function with the exact signature `int sumPair(const SameTypePair<int>& p)`.
2. Return `p.first + p.second`.
3. Do not modify the templates, the alias, or `main`.
4. The program must compile under `g++ -std=c++20 -Wall -Wextra` with no warnings.

## Notes
This exercise touches class templates (13.13), CTAD (13.14), alias templates (13.15), and passing structs by const reference (13.10).
